items.title: Global X Japan Co. Ltd. Has $2.78 Million Stake in Intel Corporation $INTC
items.content: Global X Japan Co. Ltd. reduced its stake in Intel Corporation by 27.3% in the fourth quarter, now holding 75,261 shares valued at $2.78 million. Despite a modest EPS beat, Intel's stock saw a 6.5% decline due to unclear guidance and negative net margins. The market outlook for Intel is mixed, with AI-driven CPU pricing offering potential tailwinds, but intensifying competition and execution risks remain concerns.

items.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Procore Technologies (PCOR) Powering NVIDIA’s AI Factory Digital Twin Platform
items.content: Procore Technologies recently announced an integration with NVIDIA Omniverse DSX Blueprint to create real-time digital twins for construction, especially for AI factories. This collaboration positions Procore as a potential core software layer for high-value infrastructure projects and may reshape its long-term investment narrative despite near-term risks like a fluctuating construction market and premium valuation. items.title: Amazon Chip Division Hit By Second Senior Exit In Months
items.content: Amazon's Annapurna Labs chip division has seen its second senior executive departure in recent months, with Gadi Hutt, director of product and customer engineering, leaving the company. This follows the exit of Rami Sinno in August, raising questions about leadership continuity as Amazon aims to compete with Nvidia in AI chips. Despite these leadership changes, analysts maintain a "Buy" rating for Amazon, with a consensus price target of $287.24.

items.content: F5 (FFIV) recently exceeded Q1 expectations and raised its guidance, partly due to a new alliance with Forcepoint for AI security. Despite an 11.4% share price increase over three months to US$293.15, the stock is estimated to be 26% undervalued, with a fair value of US$310.30 driven by its AI offerings and partnerships. This valuation depends on F5 maintaining software adoption and competing effectively against cloud giants.

items.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Securities Class Action Filed Against Super Micro
items.content: Kirby McInerney LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against Super Micro Computer, Inc. on behalf of investors who purchased securities between April 30, 2024, and March 19, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Super Micro illegally diverted billions of dollars worth of servers to China, violating U.S. export control laws, leading to a significant drop in its stock price. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm to discuss lead plaintiff appointment.

items.title: IWO vs. VUG: One Offers Broad Growth Exposure While the Other Has Lower Fees
items.content: This article compares two growth-focused ETFs, Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG) and iShares Russell 2000 Growth ETF (IWO), highlighting their differences in cost, portfolio composition, and performance. VUG focuses on large-cap tech stocks with lower fees and better long-term returns, while IWO offers broader diversification in small-cap growth stocks across various sectors. Investors need to weigh lower fees and performance against diversification when choosing between these two ETFs.

items.title: If You Bought Lucid, Nio, or Rivian Stock 5 Years Ago, Here’s How Much You’ve Lost
items.content: This article examines the significant losses investors would have incurred by purchasing Lucid, Nio, or Rivian stock five years ago. Lucid saw a 96.3% loss since its 2021 SPAC merger, while Nio and Rivian also experienced substantial declines of 84.6% and 84.9% respectively during similar periods. Despite initial high hopes for these EV startups, competitive pressures and execution challenges led to poor financial performance compared to the S&P 500's growth.

items.title: Lemonade Stock Eyes Fourth Straight Weekly Win: CEO Says AI Insurer Is ‘On A Tear’ And Sees Profitability This Year
items.content: Lemonade, an AI-driven insurer, is poised for its fourth consecutive weekly gain, with CEO Daniel Schreiber announcing expectations for profitability this year. The company's AI platform efficiently handles most claims and policy issuances, and it has expanded into underserved segments by attracting first-time insurance buyers. items.title: The Tesla Semi Will Cost Double a Standard Truck—but the Math Shows It Could Kill Off Diesels
items.content: Tesla is set to significantly ramp up production of its Semi truck, with plans to deliver between 5,000 and 15,000 units in 2026, eventually reaching 50,000 annually. Despite costing approximately double that of a diesel truck, Tesla suggests the Semi's operational economics, including a 50 percent lower running cost in California, will lead to a payback within the first few years. items.title: Apple Confirms End of Mac Pro Line with No Future Models Planned
items.content: Apple has officially discontinued its Mac Pro desktop computer, confirming that no future models are planned. The Mac Pro, last updated in 2023 with an M2 Ultra chip, will be replaced as Apple's high-end desktop by the Mac Studio, which features the M3 Ultra chip and is expected to receive an M5 Ultra refresh later in 2026. This move reflects Apple's strategy to simplify its desktop lineup, focusing on compact, high-performance options like the Mac Studio.

items.content: Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) has received a consensus "Hold" rating from 24 analysts, with an average 1-year price target of $70.50. Despite beating Q1 earnings expectations, the company's revenue was down year-over-year, and Q2 2026 EPS guidance was lower. Skyworks also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.71, equating to a 5.0% yield.

items.content: Masimo Corporation (NASDAQ:MASI) has received an average "Hold" recommendation from nine analysts, with an average 1-year price target of $181.17. Recent downgrades from firms like BTIG, Wolfe Research, and Piper Sandler suggest waning analyst enthusiasm, and the company's shares trade at $176.89 with a market cap of $9.5 billion. Institutional investors hold a significant portion of the company's stock, with some recent large acquisitions.

items.title: LG Display Targets OLED Monitor Market for Growth
items.content: LG Display, having successfully restructured to focus on OLEDs, faces challenges due to a high revenue dependency on a single client, believed to be Apple. To diversify and mitigate risks from potential smartphone shipment decreases and pricing pressures, the company is expanding into the growing OLED monitor market. This strategy includes unveiling advanced OLED panels for gaming monitors, with an expected increase in OLED monitor panel shipments.

items.title: Apple adds Bosch, Cirrus Logic, others to US manufacturing program, to invest $400 million
items.content: Apple is expanding its American Manufacturing Program by adding Bosch, Cirrus Logic, TDK, and Qnity Electronics, planning to invest $400 million through 2030. This initiative aims to boost U.S.-based production of key components like sensors and integrated circuits, aligning with a broader trend of shifting supply chains to reduce geopolitical risks. The expansion is expected to create jobs and strengthen domestic semiconductor and advanced electronics manufacturing capabilities.

Despite the challenges of trust and accountability in probabilistic AI systems, Intuit's GenOS is facilitating significant growth and integrating with external AI ecosystems.

items.content: This article argues that Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) is a more attractive investment than Coca-Cola (KO) based on valuation and growth metrics. KDP has a lower price-to-operating-income ratio but shows higher revenue and operating income growth compared to Coca-Cola, suggesting a potential disconnect between its valuation and performance. The analysis indicates that KDP's under-valuation relative to its peers has been a persistent trend, reinforcing the conclusion that it could be a smarter buy.

items.title: Campaign Automation Market May See a Big Move | HubSpot, Adobe, Mailchimp
items.content: The Campaign Automation Market is expanding due to increased digital transformation, demand for efficient lead generation, and the need for advanced analytics. Key players include HubSpot, Salesforce, and Adobe, with growth driven by AI-driven automation, omnichannel orchestration, and real-time personalization. Opportunities exist in advanced AI models, SME expansion, integration with emerging technologies, and growth in emerging markets.

items.content: Meta Platforms (META) recently faced two significant legal setbacks in California and New Mexico, leading to financial penalties and increased regulatory risks. Despite share prices dropping by 8%, Morningstar maintains a $850 fair value estimate, believing the market reaction is overly punitive. The firm's proactive measures, substantial financial reserves, and potential for unified federal legislation that could disadvantage smaller competitors are cited as reasons for this optimistic outlook.
